description: When multiple cloud server instances in a private network need to access the public network, you can use SNAT rules of the public NAT gateway to save public IPs and avoid exposing cloud server IPs directly to the public network for secure, convenient, and efficient access. SNAT rules support four granularities: private network, subnet, cloud server, and custom network segment. Configure flexibly based on your business needs.

EipAddress.description: IP address of the public IP associated with the SNAT rule. Returns the IP address for a single public IP. Returns IP addresses for multiple public IPs.

EipId.description: ID of the public IP associated with the SNAT rule. Returns the ID for a single public IP. Returns IDs for multiple public IPs.

NatGatewayId.description: ID of the NAT gateway associated with the SNAT rule

NatIpId.description: ID of the transit IP for the private NAT gateway

SourceCidr.description: Network segment corresponding to the SNAT rule

Status.description: Status of the SNAT rule. Creating: being created. Deleting: being deleted. Available: available.

SubnetId.description: ID of the subnet associated with the SNAT rule
